item.title: Oil Shock Sends High-Flying Materials Stocks to Rock Bottom
item.description: One of the US stock market’s biggest winners this year has turned into the worst-hit sector since the start of the Iran War as a spike in oil prices has boosted industrial production costs.

item.title: Powell Pushes Back Against Trump on Rates and Legal Investigation
item.description: The Fed governor suggested interest rates aren’t going anywhere, and neither is he

item.title: Meta Faces Threats to User Growth at the Statehouse and the Courthouse
item.description: Juries weigh the harm to children while lawmakers consider social media age restrictions

item.title: Follow ECB Rate Decision News, Analysis
item.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) headquarter. Photographer: Alex Kraus/Bloomberg

item.title: China Starts Year With Fastest Increase in Spending Since 2022
item.description: China’s government spending got off to its fastest start to any year since 2022, likely an effort by authorities to fortify the economy in the face of rising external uncertainty.

item.title: Bond Traders Are Giving Up on the Idea of Fed Rate Cuts
item.description: Money markets show chance of rate cut in 2026 is essentially a coin flip.

item.title: Europe Urged by Nobel Prize Winner to Stop Technological Decline
item.description: Europe must do more to foster innovation and stop leaning on other parts of the world for technological advancement, according to Philippe Aghion, who jointly won the 2025 Nobel Prize for economics.

item.title: UOB Cuts CEO Wee’s Pay Package by About 20% After Profit Slump
item.description: United Overseas Bank Ltd. cut Chief Executive Officer Wee Ee Cheong’s 2025 compensation by about S$3 million following a slump in its profit and dividend.

item.title: European Airlines Warn Higher Fuel Costs Will Be Paid by Flyers
item.description: Europe’s major airlines warned Thursday they’ll have to pass on higher fuel costs to their passengers as the war in Iran escalates and oil tankers remain stranded in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Japan’s Farms and Buses Struggle to Find Fuel Due to War Disruption
item.description: Japan’s cities and villages are starting to experience fuel shortages affecting everything from public transport to farming, as disruptions from the Iran war seep deeper into daily life.

item.title: Jack Ma-Backed Ant’s Profit Fell 91% on AI Spending, Fair Value
item.description: Ant Group Co.’s quarterly profit plunged 91% after the Chinese digital payments firm ramped up spending to compete in artificial intelligence and health care, while a decline in the fair value of certain investments weighed on results.

item.title: JPMorgan Strategists Say Investors Are Complacent on Iran War
item.description: Complacent investors who assume there will be a swift resolution to the Iran war are making a high-risk bet given how bad surging oil prices typically end up being for stocks, according to strategists at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: Weeks of War Are Reshaping the Global Gas Market for Years to Come
item.description: The industry is staring down prolonged disruptions in the Persian Gulf. Emerging economies are already paying the price.

item.title: Robots Need Offices Too: The AI Boom Comes to NYC Real Estate
item.description: Fast-growing startups and legacy tech firms need more space to put their human workers.

item.title: UAE Stands by $1.4 Trillion US Investment Pledge Despite War
item.description: The United Arab Emirates’ ambassador to the US threw his weight behind his country’s massive investment plans in America, amid concerns that a prolonged war with Iran could strain government finances across the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Amundi Says Markets Now See Iran War Last Months, Not Weeks
item.description: Global markets have turned sharply more pessimistic about the length of the Iran conflict and now expect it to keep going for months, Amundi SA Chief Investment Officer Vincent Mortier said.

item.title: Traders Shift BOE, ECB Rates Outlook Just Hours Before Decisions
item.description: The threat of an energy crisis drove traders to bet on more interest-rate hikes from the Bank of England and European Central Bank just hours before policymakers are due to announce their latest decisions.

item.title: Alibaba’s 67% Profit Plunge Shows Urgent Need to Monetize AI
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd.’s earnings plunged while revenue barely grew, underscoring the urgency behind the Chinese e-commerce leader’s drive to wring more profits out of a swathe of costly AI endeavors.

item.title: Norway’s Improving Business Outlook in Survey Dents Easing Bets
item.description: A key survey of Norway’s business sentiment signaled somewhat better prospects for the nation’s companies, suggesting less pressure on the central bank to consider any easing moves in the near term.

item.title: Brazil’s Ethanol Frenzy Shows Cracks as Raízen Crisis Deepens
item.description: Big oil and global crop traders rushed into Brazil’s ethanol market more than a decade ago with a spate of deals. Now, the most emblematic of those deals is unraveling amid fading enthusiasm for the business.

item.title: Billionaire Aponte’s Firm Takes Stake in Korean Tanker Giant
item.description: MSC Mediterranean Shipping Co. SA, the shipping giant founded by secretive Italian billionaire Gianluigi Aponte, bought a stake in the South Korean company that has shaken up oil tanker markets lately, the first public acknowledgment of ties between the two firms.

item.title: RBI Seeks Ways for Russia to Use Rupee Reserves, Official Says
item.description: India is exploring additional ways for Russia to utilize its accumulated rupee reserves, a senior central bank official said.

item.title: Indian Stocks Set for Worst Decline Since 2024 as Brent Spikes
item.description: Indian stocks tumbled as a surge in crude oil prices, coupled with a selloff in the nation’s top private lender dented investor sentiment.

item.title: Oil, Gas Prices Jump as Strikes on Gulf Facilities Escalate
item.description: The price of oil and natural gas jumped as escalating attacks in the Persian Gulf threatened long-term damage to major energy facilities.

item.title: The Enormous Financial Cost of Three Weeks of War in Iran
item.description: An estimated $11 billion was spent in just the first six days.

item.title: Asia Refiners Ask Saudis to Change Oil Pricing System Due to War
item.description: Asian oil refiners have asked Saudi Arabia to change the way it prices the crude they take after the Middle East war disrupted the traditional system, according to traders.

item.title: Watch Exports Bounced Back in February Before War in Middle East
item.description: Swiss watch exports returned to growth last month as US demand continued an uneven recovery from the fallout over trade tariffs.

item.title: CK Hutchison Profit Misses, With Outlook Dented By Iran War
item.description: CK Hutchison Holdings Ltd. reported a weaker-than-expected profit for 2025, even as the Iran conflict ripples through global trade and adds fresh strain to the conglomerate’s ports and retail businesses.

item.title: SNB Restates Increased Resolve on Franc as Rate Held at Zero
item.description: The Swiss National Bank restated its heightened readiness to sell the franc as officials held back again from the more drastic step of cutting borrowing costs into negative territory.

item.title: Sweden Holds Interest Rates Steady as Iran War Clouds Outlook
item.description: Sweden’s central bank held borrowing costs unchanged for a fourth straight meeting and restated a plan for no changes this year as it awaits more clarity on impacts from the war in the Middle East.
